title: MedicationRequest (active, RxNorm) -> one drug_exposure row
notes: An active MedicationRequest carrying an RxNorm-coded medicationCodeableConcept produces exactly one drug_exposure row. Source code RxNorm 896209 ('60 ACTUAT fluticasone propionate 0.25 MG/ACTUAT / salmeterol 0.05 MG/ACTUAT Dry Powder Inhaler') is itself a standard OMOP Drug concept: vocab.concept resolves it to concept_id 40169281 (vocabulary_id RxNorm, domain_id Drug, standard_concept S, concept_class Quant Clinical Drug), and concept_relationship has a self 'Maps to' (40169281 -> 40169281). The stage-2 ETL (mapspec/etl/MedicationRequest__drug_exposure.sql) takes the RxNorm coding first (NDC is fallback), joins src concept 40169281 via 'Maps to' to standard Drug concept 40169281, so drug_concept_id = 40169281 and drug_source_concept_id = src.concept_id = 40169281 (they coincide here precisely because the RxNorm clinical-drug code is already a standard concept; for a non-standard source they would differ). drug_source_value = the raw RxNorm code '896209'. drug_type_concept_id is the constant 38000177 ('Prescription written', vocabulary Drug Type). Because MedicationRequest carries no dispense info in this case, start = end = authoredOn: drug_exposure_start_date = drug_exposure_end_date = 2005-01-13 and the *_datetime pair = 2005-01-13T07:23:04 (authoredOn parsed to timestamp). person_id from subject (Patient), visit_occurrence_id from encounter (Encounter), provider_id from requester (Practitioner) -- the stage-1 view applies getReferenceKey() so the bare id is hashed via referenceToId(). The status filter keeps active/completed and drops entered-in-error/cancelled/draft/unknown (and this project additionally skips 'stopped'); 'active' passes, so the row is emitted. All other drug_exposure columns (verbatim_end_date, stop_reason, refills, quantity, days_supply, sig, route_concept_id, lot_number, visit_detail_id, route_source_value, dose_unit_source_value) are NULL -- none of their FHIR sources are present.
